item.title: Lecture | Thomas Wentworth’s Wives and Other Women
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Wednesday 14th October, 7.00pm-8.15pm Much has been written about Thomas Wentworth, first Earl of Strafford’s political alliances with other powerful men – but he also had important relationships with women, including his third wife, Elizabeth, and the great beauty of the Court, Lucy, Countess of Carlisle. Fiona Pogson will talk about how these women supported […]

The post Lecture | Thomas Wentworth’s Wives and Other Women appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday 14th October, 7.00pm-8.15pm

Much has been written about Thomas Wentworth, first Earl of Strafford’s political alliances with other powerful men – but he also had important relationships with women, including his third wife, Elizabeth, and the great beauty of the Court, Lucy, Countess of Carlisle. Fiona Pogson will talk about how these women supported his work and furthered the development of his image as a successful servant of the Crown.

Dr Fiona Pogson is a Senior Lecturer in History at Liverpool Hope University, whose research focuses on Thomas Wentworth, Earl of Strafford, and the politics of the court of Charles I. She has published widely on Wentworth’s career, the Council of the North and early Stuart politics.

Price: £15.00 (includes a welcome drink of tea or coffee)
Accompanying Carer: Free

Lecture Series Offer

Buy tickets to one lecture or an elevenses lecture in our series and get 50% off tickets to a second of your choice! Discount will automatically be applied at checkout.

Accessibility

This event takes place in the Low Drawing Room, which is located on the ground floor of the House and is fully accessible.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our events goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House and Gardens.

The post Lecture | Thomas Wentworth’s Wives and Other Women appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Elevenses Lecture | Restoration of the South Tower
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Tuesday 8th September, 11.00am Join Caroline Stamford, in-house historian for the Landmark Trust, to learn more about the ongoing restoration of the South Tower. Price: £15.00 (Includes welcome drink of tea or coffee)Accompanying Carer: Free Lecture Series Offer Buy tickets to one lecture or an elevenses lecture in our series and get 50% off tickets […]

The post Elevenses Lecture | Restoration of the South Tower appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday 8th September, 11.00am

Join Caroline Stamford, in-house historian for the Landmark Trust, to learn more about the ongoing restoration of the South Tower.

Price: £15.00 (Includes welcome drink of tea or coffee)
Accompanying Carer: Free

Lecture Series Offer

Buy tickets to one lecture or an elevenses lecture in our series and get 50% off tickets to a second of your choice! Discount will automatically be applied at checkout.

Accessibility

This event takes place in the Low Drawing Room, which is located on the ground floor of the House and is fully accessible.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our events goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House and Gardens.

The post Elevenses Lecture | Restoration of the South Tower appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Rotherham Hospice | Meadow of Memories Butterfly Appeal
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Saturday 4th to Sunday 19th July, 10.00am-5.00pm This summer, Rotherham Hospice invites you to dedicate a butterfly in memory of someone you love. As part of their Butterfly Appeal, thousands of beautiful steel butterfly ornaments, crafted by MTL Advanced, will come together in the Gardens of Wentworth Woodhouse, creating a powerful and peaceful tribute — a Meadow of […]

The post Rotherham Hospice | Meadow of Memories Butterfly Appeal appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Saturday 4th to Sunday 19th July, 10.00am-5.00pm

This summer, Rotherham Hospice invites you to dedicate a butterfly in memory of someone you love. As part of their Butterfly Appeal, thousands of beautiful steel butterfly ornaments, crafted by MTL Advanced, will come together in the Gardens of Wentworth Woodhouse, creating a powerful and peaceful tribute — a Meadow of Memories.

Each butterfly tells a story. Each one holds a wish. By dedicating a butterfly, you’ll honour their memory in a truly meaningful way, while helping Rotherham Hospice continue to add more life to every day for patients and families in our care.

Suggested Donation to Rotherham Hospice: £40.00
Garden Admission: General Admission

Find Out More

Accessibility

The Meadow of Memories will be located within the meadow in the Gardens here at Wentworth Woodhouse. The Gardens are fully accessible. Well behaved dogs on leads are also welcome to join their human friends to explore the Gardens.

The post Rotherham Hospice | Meadow of Memories Butterfly Appeal appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: South Yorkshire Classic Car and Motorcycle Show
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Sunday 2nd August, 10.00am-4.00pm Start your engines! We’ll be welcoming back the South Yorkshire Classic Car and Motorcycle Show this summer for another spectacular day of motoring heritage – ideal for enthusiasts, families, and day trippers. From a full show of vehicles and trade and autojumble stalls, to modern car dealership stands and quality food […]

The post South Yorkshire Classic Car and Motorcycle Show appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Sunday 2nd August, 10.00am-4.00pm

Start your engines!

We’ll be welcoming back the South Yorkshire Classic Car and Motorcycle Show this summer for another spectacular day of motoring heritage – ideal for enthusiasts, families, and day trippers. From a full show of vehicles and trade and autojumble stalls, to modern car dealership stands and quality food and drink, there’s something for everyone to enjoy!

Adult (15+): £12.00
Child (3-14): £5.50
Family (2+ up to 3): £29.50

Accessibility

This event takes place on the East Front Lawn and so is fully accessible, but please note there may be some uneven surfaces.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from events goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post South Yorkshire Classic Car and Motorcycle Show appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Saturday 12th September, 10.00am-4.30pm History comes alive at the Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival – a day packed with brilliant talks, fascinating stories, live entertainment, and hands-on experience in one of Britain’s grandest historic houses. Hear from acclaimed historians, authors and podcast hosts including Kate Lister (Betwixt the Sheets, The Curious History of Sex, Flick), Maddy […]

The post Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Saturday 12th September, 10.00am-4.30pm

History comes alive at the Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival – a day packed with brilliant talks, fascinating stories, live entertainment, and hands-on experience in one of Britain’s grandest historic houses.

Hear from acclaimed historians, authors and podcast hosts including Kate Lister (Betwixt the Sheets, The Curious History of Sex, Flick), Maddy Pelling (After Dark, Writing on the Wall, Hoax), Anthony Delaney (After Dark, Queer Georgians), and Catherine Bailey (Black Diamonds). Alongside the talks, explore living history displays with Tastes Through Time and military re-enactors. You can also try your hand at heritage craft workshops, and enjoy live music from Satnam Galsian, Maddie Morris, and 1940s vocalist, Miss Sarah Jane

Whether you’re passionate about social history, hidden stories, historic houses, or simply love a great day out, the Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival offers a unique chance to immerse yourself in the past.

Early Bird Prices (Valid until Sunday 28th June)

Adult (17+): £62.50
Child (3-16): £12.50

Standard Prices

Adult (17+): £65.00
Child (3-16): £13.50

All tickets include access to the House & Gardens (usually £16.50).

What’s On

Talks

Dr Kate Lister

Dr Kate Lister is a historian and author specialising in the history of sex, scandal, and intimate lives throughout history. She is the presenter of the popular ‘Betwixt the Sheets’ podcast and author of books including, ‘The Curious History of Sex’ and ‘Flick’, bringing hidden and often surprising stories from the past to life.

Dr Madeleine Pelling

Dr Maddy Pelling is a historian, writer, and broadcaster specialising in the cultural and social history of the medieval and early modern world. She is co-host of the popular ‘After Dark’ podcast and author of books including ‘Writing on the Wall’ and ‘Hoax’, exploring everything from forgotten histories to deception, belief, and everyday life in the past.

Dr Anthony Delaney

Dr Anthony Delaney is a historian, broadcaster, actor and presenter known for bringing compelling historical narratives to life across screen and audio. Originally from Kilkenny, Ireland, he holds a PhD in 18th-century social and cultural history from the University of Exeter, where he is an Honorary Fellow. Anthony co-hosts the award-winning podcast After Dark: Myths, Misdeeds & the Paranormal for History Hit and has presented documentaries including The Edinburgh Murders: Burke and Hare, The Trial of Oscar Wilde and Death and the Brontës. His debut book, Queer Georgians: A Hidden History, was published in 2025 by Doubleday UK and Grove Atlantic US.

Catherine Bailey

Catherine Bailey is an award-winning historian and bestselling author known for uncovering the hidden stories behind Britain’s great estates and aristocratic families. Her acclaimed works, including ‘Black Diamonds’, combine meticulous research with gripping storytelling to bring the dramas of the past vividly to life.

Performances

Satnam Galsain

Satnam Galsian is a British Asian singer-songwriter and progressive folk artist from Leeds, whose music blends the rich tradition of Punjabi folk with contemporary, feminist storytelling.

Maddie Morris

Rooted in the oral tradition, Maddie’s music seeks to preserve, challenge, and expand folk heritage by writing historically excluded voices into the canon. Passionately political yet gently understated, this is music that deftly treads the path between art and activism.

Miss Sarah-Jane

Miss Sarah-Jane is a UK-based 1940s tribute artist and vocalist specialising in wartime, vintage, and swing music, often performing in a replica WAAF uniform.

Workshops

Tastes Through Time

Tastes Through Time will bring history alive through immersive displays and captivating talks that explore the rich tapestry of food and cooking. In their displays, you’ll meet Countess Maude Fitzwilliam to explore the responsibilities of managing a large country house, and Katherine Moffat, nanny to the children of the household.

Natural Dyes and Tote Bags | Charlie Hill

Charlie is a creative outdoor facilitator and artist whose work explores ways to connect to nature and each other through community, creativity, and bushcraft.

Dance the Past

Step back in time and discover the social dances of the Regency period in this introductory one-hour workshop. Drawing on surviving dance manuals, you’ll learn a selection of simple dances enjoyed in the ballrooms of Jane Austen’s day while exploring the role of dance in the social life of the era. No previous dance experience is required and no partner is needed – just bring your enthusiasm and your dancing feet!

Additional Information

  • Each workshop and talk has a capacity so will be limited to first come first served on the day
  • Redeem your tickets at front desk, where you’ll receive a timetable
  • Workshops and talks take place throughout the day
  • Living History displays are in the Garden

Accessibility

Activities in the State Rooms are accessible using the temporary lift. The Garden is accessible but some areas include displays on grass and on uneven pathways. Contact info@wentworthwoodhouse.org.uk for specific access enquiries.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our events goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Wentworth Woodhouse History Festival appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Goldsmiths North
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Friday 13th to Sunday 15th November, 10.00am-4.00pm This autumn, Goldsmiths North and Wentworth Woodhouse will be collaborating for the first time to hold a unique event in our magnificent House. Specially selected Goldsmiths North exhibitors will be showing and selling their beautiful, handmade silverware and jewellery at Wentworth Woodhouse from Friday 13th to Sunday 15th […]

The post Goldsmiths North appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Friday 13th to Sunday 15th November, 10.00am-4.00pm

This autumn, Goldsmiths North and Wentworth Woodhouse will be collaborating for the first time to hold a unique event in our magnificent House.

Specially selected Goldsmiths North exhibitors will be showing and selling their beautiful, handmade silverware and jewellery at Wentworth Woodhouse from Friday 13th to Sunday 15th November – perfect for Christmas shopping.

Price: £19.50 (inc. House & Garden general admission)

Elevate Your Day

Why not elevate your day and join us in for Afternoon Tea in the Long Gallery and receive 50% off your House & Garden admission?* Indulge in a delightful selection of delicate finger sandwiches, freshly baked scones served with clotted cream and preserves, and an array of irresistible sweet treats – thoughtfully prepared to capture the flavours of the season.

*Discount automatically applied at checkout when you have a Goldsmiths North ticket in your basket

Accessibility

A temporary lift provides access to the first-floor State Rooms and exhibition space for visitors with mobility impairments. A staff member or volunteer will accompany visitors using the lift.  For queries, contact info@wentworthwoodhouse.org.uk.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from events goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Goldsmiths North appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Afternoon Tea in the Long Gallery
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Join us in The Long Gallery throughout the year for a truly special Afternoon Tea experience. As the seasons change, so do our menus using the finest local produce. From the delicate florals of spring to the warm, comforting flavours of winter, each menu reflects the very best of the season – the perfect treat […]

The post Afternoon Tea in the Long Gallery appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Join us in The Long Gallery throughout the year for a truly special Afternoon Tea experience.

As the seasons change, so do our menus using the finest local produce. From the delicate florals of spring to the warm, comforting flavours of winter, each menu reflects the very best of the season – the perfect treat for you and your loved ones.

Current Menu | Summer Afternoon Tea

Friday 29th May to Sunday 30th August
Timed sittings between 11.30am-2.30pm

Soak in the summer in the Long Gallery and celebrate the warmth of the season with a delicious Afternoon Tea. Indulge in a delightful selection of delicate finger sandwiches, freshly baked scones served with clotted cream and preserves, and an array of irresistible summer treats – thoughtfully prepared to capture the fun and fresh flavours of the season.

View the Summer Afternoon Tea menu HERE.

Book Now

Coming Soon… | Autumn Afternoon Tea

Saturday 5th September to Sunday 15th November
Timed sittings between 11.00am-2.00pm

Embrace the changing colours of the season in the Long Gallery and enjoy a comforting Autumn Afternoon Tea filled with seasonal delights.

Book Now

Great British Summer Savings

This summer, we’re proud to be participating in the Great British Summer Savings scheme! That means we’re cutting our prices and giving away some great offers so that you can visit the Big House with Bigger Savings! Valid on sittings up to Tuesday 1st September.

Get 10% off children’s menus (inc. Afternoon Tea)!*

Treat your family’s tiny tums to some delicious treats and time together at any of our catering outlets, from a quick pitstop at the Butler’s Pantry Café to a brilliant brunch in the Camellia House surrounded by Garden blooms and indulgent Afternoon Teas in the Long Gallery.

*Discount automatically applied to ticket type

Accessibility

Due to the age and nature of the House, some areas are only accessible via stairs and there are some uneven surfaces. A temporary lift provides access to the first-floor for visitors with mobility impairments. There is then a ramp in use down two steps to access the Long Gallery. A staff member or volunteer will accompany visitors using the lift. For queries, contact info@wentworthwoodhouse.org.uk.

House & Gardens Discount

When you book a Long Gallery Afternoon Tea you will also receive a 50% discount on House & Gardens admission for the day of your visit. Simply add this to your cart when booking your Afternoon Tea.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our Afternoon Tea goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes towards the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Afternoon Tea in the Long Gallery appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Summer Holidays @ The Craft Cabin
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Friday to Sunday from Saturday 18th July, 11.00am-3.00pm Get creative this summer holidays at the Craft Cabin here at Wentworth Woodhouse! From seasonal crafts to hands-on activities for all ages, this is the perfect way to spend a relaxed summer afternoon. Drop in Friday to Sunday, get creative, and take home something special you’ve made […]

The post Summer Holidays @ The Craft Cabin appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Friday to Sunday from Saturday 18th July, 11.00am-3.00pm

Get creative this summer holidays at the Craft Cabin here at Wentworth Woodhouse! From seasonal crafts to hands-on activities for all ages, this is the perfect way to spend a relaxed summer afternoon.

Drop in Friday to Sunday, get creative, and take home something special you’ve made yourself!

Adult: General Admission
Child: Free*
Accompanying Carer: Free

Please note, some activities may incur a small charge.

Accessibility

The Craft Cabin is located on the West Front Lawn so is fully accessible.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our activities goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes to the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Summer Holidays @ The Craft Cabin appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Exhibition | Quilts for the Longest Day: A Sewing Group’s Accidental Journey
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Tuesday 21st July to Saturday 14th November, 10.00am-5.00pm Every stitch counts… Quilts for the Longest Day began as a simple idea in a local fabric shop in Chapletown and grew into a powerful community movement to support veterans. What started as a single quilt quickly expanded into an ambitious effort to create 80. Surpassing expectations, […]

The post Exhibition | Quilts for the Longest Day: A Sewing Group’s Accidental Journey appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday 21st July to Saturday 14th November, 10.00am-5.00pm

Every stitch counts…

Quilts for the Longest Day began as a simple idea in a local fabric shop in Chapletown and grew into a powerful community movement to support veterans. What started as a single quilt quickly expanded into an ambitious effort to create 80. Surpassing expectations, in 2024 alone, 109 handmade quilts were gifted to veterans.

Rooted in collaboration, care, and gratitude, this project has continued to grow as a not-for-profit, delivering comfort and recognition to those who have served with over 126 quilts donated to date.

Now, a selection of these quilts are coming to our Community Gallery space in the Old Hall, celebrating both the craftsmanship and the stories behind this remarkable initiative.

Adult: General Admission
Child: Free
Accompanying Carer: Free

Accessibility

This exhibition takes place in the Old Hall which is located on the ground floor of the House making it fully accessible for visitors.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our exhibitions goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes to the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Exhibition | Quilts for the Longest Day: A Sewing Group’s Accidental Journey appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.

item.title: Exhibition | Echoes of the House: Stories in Light and Motion
item.dc:creator: Emily Davidson
item.description:
Tuesday 7th July to Sunday 8th November, 10.00am-5.00pm Step inside our house of memories… Echoes of the House: Stories in Light and Motion will reimagine Wentworth Woodhouse’s State Rooms as a prism of memories using immersive digital storytelling that blends historic cinematic devices – from zoetropes to magic lanterns – with contemporary art installations. Journey […]

The post Exhibition | Echoes of the House: Stories in Light and Motion appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday 7th July to Sunday 8th November, 10.00am-5.00pm

Step inside our house of memories…

Echoes of the House: Stories in Light and Motion will reimagine Wentworth Woodhouse’s State Rooms as a prism of memories using immersive digital storytelling that blends historic cinematic devices – from zoetropes to magic lanterns – with contemporary art installations.

Journey through a house where memories come alive in light. As you explore, you’ll encounter stories from the past and present, woven together with personal reflections and community voices to reveal the many lives and experiences connected to this place.

These are your stories. Real stories of real people.

Adult: General Admission
Child: Free
Accompanying Carer: Free

Accessibility

A temporary lift provides access to the first-floor State Rooms for visitors with mobility impairments. A staff member or volunteer will accompany visitors using the lift. For queries, contact info@wentworthwoodhouse.org.uk.

Help Support Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust

Remember, any money made from our exhibitions goes straight into the Wentworth Woodhouse Preservation Trust and contributes to the restoration of this magnificent House & Gardens.

The post Exhibition | Echoes of the House: Stories in Light and Motion appeared first on Wentworth Woodhouse.
